mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient rewards matching-prefix progress on SQL keyword and shift-operator comparisons, preserving partial-progress seeds; both decisive pairs add exactly value_profile, isolating CMP_MAP gradient as the enabling technique.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient assembles SELECT-plus-shift-operator SQL, both decisive pairs implicate value_profile

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP distance-bucket signal preserves partial-prefix SELECT/shift-operator inputs that naive discards; Hamming/prefix-distance bucketing on tokenizer CMPs ratchets toward a parseable '>>' expression where naive stalls.
one_line: CMP_MAP distance-bucket ratchets toward parseable '>>' shift-operator SQL expression

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP writes an operand-distance-bucket per CMP; an input closer to matching keyword/operator tokens adds a new bucket and is kept, giving a gradient toward bytes that compile OP_Lt.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient preserves partial-keyword seeds climbing toward OP_Lt compilation

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient rewards near-miss matches on tokenizer/parser comparisons for the dotted-name structure; each closer-than-seen distance adds a new bucket, incrementally assembling the rare two-dot X.Y.Z qualified-name layout.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient incrementally assembles two-dot X.Y.Z qualified-name token structure

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP intercepts sqlite3StrICmp schema-name comparisons; a token Hamming-closer to a real table/column name adds a new bucket and is preserved, climbing toward a match that increments cntTab.
one_line: CMP_MAP gradient climbs toward schema-matching table.column token incrementing cntTab

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient rewards incremental Hamming progress on string comparisons against db/schema names during dotted X.Y.Z token parsing, preserving partial-match seeds that climb toward the three-part qualifier plain cmplog cannot assemble.
one_line: VP gradient climbs string CMPs toward three-part schema-qualified SQL name

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs whose tokenizer character comparisons get closer to producing the '.' byte that classifies an expression as TK_DOT, preserving partial-prefix seeds naive discards.
one_line: VP gradient steers tokenizer toward '.' byte needed for TK_DOT dispatch

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP records a Hamming/prefix-distance bucket per integer comparison; an input bringing a literal comparison closer to the true-side constant adds a new bucket and is preserved, providing a gradient toward integer-literal arithmetic structure the codegen branch needs.
one_line: VP gradient preserves near-miss integer-literal seeds for codegen branch

mechanism_summary: VP intercepts integer CMPs inside the expression evaluator and writes a distance-bucket CMP_MAP entry; inputs whose arithmetic operand is closer to the true-side constant are preserved, climbing toward the deeply nested operator-arithmetic SQL that reaches the codegen arm.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient climbs arithmetic-operand comparisons inside expression codegen

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient feedback preserves inputs whose relational/integer CMP operands are closer to matching, enabling incremental assembly of operator-chain SQL expressions that reach the expression-codegen arm; naive and cmplog stall without this distance signal.
one_line: VP gradient incrementally builds operator-expression SQL reaching codegen arm

mechanism_summary: CMP_MAP gradient gives Hamming/prefix-distance credit for each SQLite lexer/parser integer or string comparison; seeds closer to matching the chained comparisons along the parser path to sqlite3TwoPartName are preserved, letting VP climb the multi-comparison gate naive and cmplog cannot gradient toward.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient climbs chained lexer/parser CMPs to reach sqlite3TwoPartName

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP records operand-distance buckets for name-resolution comparisons inside selectExpander; inputs whose column-reference bytes drift closer to forming a matching identifier.*  wildcard token are retained, giving a gradient naive's plain edge feedback cannot follow.
one_line: VP gradient retains near-miss identifier tokens to reach wildcard expansion branch

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient preserves inputs whose lexer/parser comparison operands incrementally approach the 'vacuum' keyword and following token structure, letting VP climb the multi-comparison parse path to sqlite3Vacuum's true branch; naive and cmplog lack this distance bucket feedback.
one_line: VP gradient climbs lexer/parser CMPs toward VACUUM keyword parse path

mechanism_summary: VP CMP_MAP records matching-prefix-length buckets for tokenizer/keyword CMPs; inputs one byte closer to spelling SELECT and subsequent expression tokens earn a new bucket and are preserved, ratcheting bytes toward the valid SELECT-expression prefix that drives the yy_reduce parser action.
one_line: VP prefix-distance gradient ratchets bytes toward SELECT keyword for parser reduction

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient feedback credits partial keyword matches (e.g. progressively matching 'vacuum') with new coverage buckets, preserving seeds that converge on the token strings the LALR grammar needs; naive and cmplog lack CMP_MAP and cannot gradient toward keyword comparisons.
one_line: VP Hamming gradient on keyword CMPs drives LALR parser to the reduce action

mechanism_summary: VP intercepts character/keyword comparisons in sqlite3GetToken and writes operand-distance CMP_MAP buckets; inputs whose bytes are Hamming-closer to valid SQL token characters earn new buckets and are preserved, giving a gradient toward structured SQL that naive lacks.
one_line: VP CMP_MAP gradient steers random bytes toward valid SQL token characters

mechanism_summary: VP CMP_MAP gradient buckets reward partial Hamming/prefix progress on the chained byte comparisons z[0]=='0', z[1]=='x'/'X', isxdigit(z[2]), guiding the corpus to assemble the multi-byte '0x' hex prefix that pure cmplog's edge-bucket feedback cannot reward incrementally.
one_line: VP gradient climbs chained byte checks to assemble hex-literal '0x' prefix

mechanism_summary: VP adds a CMP_MAP keyed by (PC, operand-distance bucket): each tokenizer character/keyword CMP that gets closer to matching adds a new bucket and the near-miss input is preserved, giving a Hamming/prefix-distance gradient that ratchets bytes toward valid identifier/keyword tokens; naive has no such gradient.
one_line: VP CMP gradient retains near-miss token bytes to reach tokenizer true branch

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP Hamming/prefix-distance feedback rewards inputs whose tokenizer/parser comparisons get progressively closer to forming the '>=' two-char operator token in a comparison context, giving a gradient toward the TK_GE dispatch that naive's plain edge-counter feedback cannot follow.
one_line: VP gradient climbs tokenizer CMPs toward '>=' operator token for TK_GE dispatch

mechanism_summary: VP's CMP_MAP gradient intercepts the tokenizer's second-character comparison against '=', preserving inputs whose byte is operand-closer to '=' (0x3d) than any seen before; this distance-bucket signal climbs until '=' lands right after '>/<' while naive's edge-hit feedback cannot steer toward the exact adjacent byte.
one_line: VP operand-distance gradient steers byte after '>'/'<' to exact '=' match
